title: Dockets
text: get_dockets("[agency_acronym]") retrieves dockets agency (see official acronyms regulations.gov)
code: # you need an API key #source("~/api-key.R")  library(regulationsdotgov) # get FBI dockets #FBI_dockets <- get_dockets("FBI")

title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: can install development version regulationsdotgov GitHub :
code: if (!requireNamespace('pak')) install.packages('pak') pak::pak("judgelord/regulationsdotgov") library(regulationsdotgov)

title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: need API key, can register one https://open.gsa.gov/api/regulationsgov/. want store API key locally follows:
code: api_keys <- "123tHiSkEyIsFaKe"

title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: Regulations.gov metadata organized hierarchical database. Comment Details comment (detailed metadata) regulationsdotgov package organized parallel set functions retrieve metadata:

previous_headings: Basic usage illustrated by retrieving FBI dockets, documents, and comments
title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: get_dockets("[agency_acronym]") retrieves dockets agency (see official acronyms regulations.gov)
code: # get FBI dockets FBI_dockets <- get_dockets(agency = "FBI", api_keys = api_keys)  head(FBI_dockets) # A tibble: 6 × 8    docketType    lastModifiedDate     highlightedContent agencyId title                objectId id    lastpage   <chr>         <chr>                <lgl>              <chr>    <chr>                <chr>    <chr> <lgl>    1 Rulemaking    2024-08-26T13:14:32Z NA                 FBI      "Bipartisan Safer C… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE     2 Rulemaking    2024-08-26T13:13:45Z NA                 FBI      "Child Protection I… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE     3 Rulemaking    2023-08-25T08:08:48Z NA                 FBI      "Recently Posted FB… 0b00006… FBI_… TRUE     4 Rulemaking    2021-05-02T01:06:49Z NA                 FBI      "National Instant C… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE     5 Rulemaking    2021-05-02T01:06:49Z NA                 FBI      "FBI Criminal Justi… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE     6 Nonrulemaking 2021-05-02T01:06:49Z NA                 FBI      "FBI Records Manage… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE FBI_dockets$id > FBI_dockets$id [1] "FBI-2024-0001"  "FBI-2023-0001"  "FBI_FRDOC_0001" "FBI-2013-0001"  "FBI-2008-0001"  "FBI-2008-0003"  "FBI-2016-0001"  "FBI-2008-0002" "FBI-2011-0001"

previous_headings: Basic usage illustrated by retrieving FBI dockets, documents, and comments
what: Get documents from a docket folder
title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: get_documents("[docketId]") retrieves documents docket
code: FBI_docket_2013 <- get_documents(docketId = "FBI-2013-0001", api_keys = api_keys)  head(FBI_docket_2013) > head(FBI_docket_2013) # A tibble: 2 × 17   documentType  lastModifiedDate  highlightedContent frDocNum withdrawn agencyId allowLateComment commentEndDate title postedDate docketId subtype   <chr>         <chr>             <chr>              <chr>    <lgl>     <chr>    <lgl>            <chr>          <chr> <chr>      <chr>    <lgl>   1 Proposed Rule 2013-03-30T02:01… ""                 2013-01… FALSE     FBI      FALSE            2013-03-30T03… Nati… 2013-01-2… FBI-201… NA      2 Notice        2013-03-22T20:22… ""                 NA       FALSE     FBI      FALSE            NA             Dock… 2013-03-2… FBI-201… NA      # ℹ 5 more variables: commentStartDate <chr>, openForComment <lgl>, objectId <chr>, id <chr>, lastpage <lgl> # Although the docket contains two documents, only the 'Proposed Rule' had a comment period, so we'll use the objectId for that document to collect comments. FBI_docket_2013$objectId[1] > FBI_docket_2013$objectId[1] [1] "09000064811daace"

previous_headings: Basic usage illustrated by retrieving FBI dockets, documents, and comments
what: Get metadata for all comments on a document
title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: get_commentsOnId("[objectId]") retrieves comments specific document (e.g., specific proposed rule)
code: commentsOn_FBI_docket_2013 <- get_commentsOnId(commentOnId = "09000064811daace", api_keys = api_keys)  # There are 36 comments on this document, we can see the metadata for the first 6 below head(commentsOn_FBI_docket_2013) > head(commentsOn_FBI_docket_2013) # A tibble: 6 × 11   documentType      lastModifiedDate     highlightedContent withdrawn agencyId title                objectId postedDate id    lastpage commentOnId   <chr>             <chr>                <chr>              <lgl>     <chr>    <chr>                <chr>    <chr>      <chr> <lgl>    <chr>       1 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:16:07Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 2 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:15:49Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 3 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:15:25Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 4 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:15:07Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 5 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:14:34Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 6 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:14:13Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… # Now we'll store the id for each comment on the document  comments_to_collect <- commentsOn_FBI_docket_2013$id  head(comments_to_collect) > head(comments_to_collect) [1] "FBI-2013-0001-0038" "FBI-2013-0001-0037" "FBI-2013-0001-0036" "FBI-2013-0001-0035" "FBI-2013-0001-0034" "FBI-2013-0001-0033"

previous_headings: Basic usage illustrated by retrieving FBI dockets, documents, and comments
what: Get detailed metadata about individual comments
title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: get_comment_details_content("[id]") retrieves comments specific document (e.g., specific proposed rule)
code: comments <- get_comment_details(id = comments_to_collect, api_keys = api_keys)  # We retrieve 21 attributes for these 36 comments   colnames(comments) > colnames(comment)  [1] "commentOn"           "commentOnDocumentId" "duplicateComments"   "agencyId"            "comment"             "docketId"             [7] "documentType"        "objectId"            "modifyDate"          "organization"        "pageCount"           "postedDate"          [13] "receiveDate"         "title"               "trackingNbr"         "withdrawn"           "openForComment"      "id"                  [19] "attachments" # Let's take a look a closer look at one of the comments.  comments$comment[23] > comments$comment[23] [1] "Docket No. FBI 152, Proposal 1 should be approved to stem gang violence and prevent youth suicide on Indian reservations." # We can use the following attachments attribute to download any file(s) that may accompany the comment.  comments$attachments[23] > comments$attachments[23] [[1]] [[1]][[1]]                                                                  fileUrl format   size 1 https://downloads.regulations.gov/FBI-2013-0001-0015/attachment_1.docx   docx 153789 2  https://downloads.regulations.gov/FBI-2013-0001-0015/attachment_1.pdf    pdf  74106

title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: package also contains functions use API’s search function search text documents comments: get_searchTerm(searchTerm = "[searchTerm]", documents = c("documents", "comments"), agency_acronym = c("", "[agency_acronym]"), docket_id = c("", "[docket_id]"), document_id = c("", "[document_id]")  )

title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: Finally, using URLs returned get_documents() get_comment_details(), can download files (e.g., pdfs) download_regulationsdotgov("[url]") saves local copies desired files standard file structure mirroring “files” folder: “files/agency_acronym/docket_id/document_id/document_name.extension” (e.g., “files/ACF/ACF-2009-0005/ACF-2009-0005-0018_1.doc” public comment ACF Notice ACF-2009-0005-0002. notice located “files/ACF/ACF-2009-0005/ACF-2009-0005-0002.pdf”) specify file structure, simply use base download function download.file(url = "[url]", destfile = "[path]")

title: Getting started with regulationsdotgov
text: use regulationsdotgov, need API key. keys limits. can request rate increase. functions currently built theory user wants results given agency/docket/document. highlighted documentation, user can limit search results given date lastModifiedDate argument (API calls currently hard-coded descending order). future, add options restrict results given timeframe.

title: Get Data from Regulations.gov
text: regulationsdotgov package help researchers easily retrieve data regulations.gov.

title: Get Data from Regulations.gov
text: can install development version regulationsdotgov GitHub :
code: if (!requireNamespace('pak')) install.packages('pak') pak::pak("judgelord/regulationsdotgov") library(regulationsdotgov)

title: Get Data from Regulations.gov
text: Regulations.gov metadata organized hierarchical database. Comment Details comment (detailed metadata) regulationsdotgov package organized parallel set functions retrieve metadata:

title: Get Data from Regulations.gov
text: get_dockets("[agency_acronym]") retrieves dockets agency (see official acronyms regulations.gov)
code: # You need an API key, you can register for one at https://open.gsa.gov/api/regulationsgov/.  api_keys <- "123tHiSkEyIsFaKe"  # get FBI dockets FBI_dockets <- get_dockets(agency = "FBI", api_keys = api_keys)  head(FBI_dockets) > head(FBI_dockets) # A tibble: 6 × 8    docketType    lastModifiedDate     highlightedContent agencyId title                objectId id    lastpage   <chr>         <chr>                <lgl>              <chr>    <chr>                <chr>    <chr> <lgl>    1 Rulemaking    2024-08-26T13:14:32Z NA                 FBI      "Bipartisan Safer C… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE     2 Rulemaking    2024-08-26T13:13:45Z NA                 FBI      "Child Protection I… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE     3 Rulemaking    2023-08-25T08:08:48Z NA                 FBI      "Recently Posted FB… 0b00006… FBI_… TRUE     4 Rulemaking    2021-05-02T01:06:49Z NA                 FBI      "National Instant C… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE     5 Rulemaking    2021-05-02T01:06:49Z NA                 FBI      "FBI Criminal Justi… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE     6 Nonrulemaking 2021-05-02T01:06:49Z NA                 FBI      "FBI Records Manage… 0b00006… FBI-… TRUE FBI_dockets$id > FBI_dockets$id [1] "FBI-2024-0001"  "FBI-2023-0001"  "FBI_FRDOC_0001" "FBI-2013-0001"  "FBI-2008-0001"  "FBI-2008-0003"  "FBI-2016-0001"  "FBI-2008-0002" "FBI-2011-0001"

what: Get documents from a docket folder
title: Get Data from Regulations.gov
text: get_documents("[docketId]") retrieves documents docket
code: FBI_docket_2013 <- get_documents(docketId = "FBI-2013-0001", api_keys = api_keys)  head(FBI_docket_2013) > head(FBI_docket_2013) # A tibble: 2 × 17   documentType  lastModifiedDate  highlightedContent frDocNum withdrawn agencyId allowLateComment commentEndDate title postedDate docketId subtype   <chr>         <chr>             <chr>              <chr>    <lgl>     <chr>    <lgl>            <chr>          <chr> <chr>      <chr>    <lgl>   1 Proposed Rule 2013-03-30T02:01… ""                 2013-01… FALSE     FBI      FALSE            2013-03-30T03… Nati… 2013-01-2… FBI-201… NA      2 Notice        2013-03-22T20:22… ""                 NA       FALSE     FBI      FALSE            NA             Dock… 2013-03-2… FBI-201… NA      # ℹ 5 more variables: commentStartDate <chr>, openForComment <lgl>, objectId <chr>, id <chr>, lastpage <lgl> # Although the docket contains two documents, only the 'Proposed Rule' had a comment period, so we'll use the objectId for that document to collect comments. FBI_docket_2013$objectId[1] > FBI_docket_2013$objectId[1] [1] "09000064811daace"

what: Get metadata for all comments on a document
title: Get Data from Regulations.gov
text: get_commentsOnId("[objectId]") retrieves comments specific document (e.g., specific proposed rule)
code: commentsOn_FBI_docket_2013 <- get_commentsOnId(commentOnId = "09000064811daace", api_keys = api_keys)  # There are 36 comments on this document, we can see the metadata for the first 6 below head(commentsOn_FBI_docket_2013) > head(commentsOn_FBI_docket_2013) # A tibble: 6 × 11   documentType      lastModifiedDate     highlightedContent withdrawn agencyId title                objectId postedDate id    lastpage commentOnId   <chr>             <chr>                <chr>              <lgl>     <chr>    <chr>                <chr>    <chr>      <chr> <lgl>    <chr>       1 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:16:07Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 2 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:15:49Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 3 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:15:25Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 4 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:15:07Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 5 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:14:34Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… 6 Public Submission 2013-04-01T17:14:13Z ""                 FALSE     FBI      Comment on FR Doc #… 0900006… 2013-04-0… FBI-… TRUE     0900006481… # Now we'll store the id for each comment on the document  comments_to_collect <- commentsOn_FBI_docket_2013$id  head(comments_to_collect) > head(comments_to_collect) [1] "FBI-2013-0001-0038" "FBI-2013-0001-0037" "FBI-2013-0001-0036" "FBI-2013-0001-0035" "FBI-2013-0001-0034" "FBI-2013-0001-0033"

text: use regulationsdotgov, need API key. keys limits. can request rate increase. functions currently built theory user wants results given agency/docket/document. highlighted documentation, user can limit search results given date lastModifiedDate argument (API calls currently hard-coded descending order). future, add options restrict results given timeframe.

what: Retrieve Full Comment Details and Content — get_comment_details
title: Retrieve Full Comment Details and Content — get_comment_details
text: Fetches complete comment details content regulations.gov using comment IDs. Returns data frame containing full text, metadata, attachment information requested comment.

title: Retrieve Full Comment Details and Content — get_comment_details
code: get_comment_details(id, lastModifiedDate = Sys.time(), api_keys)

title: Retrieve Full Comment Details and Content — get_comment_details
text: id Character string vector containing comment ID(s) obtained get_commentsOnId search functions. Duplicate IDs automatically removed optimize API usage. lastModifiedDate Filter comments last modified date. Default current system time. Format must : "yyyy-MM-dd%20HH:mm:ss" (e.g., "2024-01-01%2000:00:00" January 1, 2024). api_keys Character string vector containing API key(s) api.data.gov. multiple keys provided, function cycle manage rate limits.

title: Retrieve Full Comment Details and Content — get_comment_details
text: function retrieves full content public comments, including comment text, submitter information, attachments. Unlike get_commentsOnId returns metadata, function fetches complete comment details regulations.gov API. function encounters errors execution, save successfully retrieved comments temporary .rda file provide file path recovery.

title: Retrieve Full Comment Details and Content — get_comment_details
text: Regulations.gov API Documentation: https://open.gsa.gov/api/regulationsgov/

title: Retrieve Full Comment Details and Content — get_comment_details
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get details for a single comment using its ID comment_details <- get_comment_details(   id = "FBI-2024-0001-0002",    api_keys = "DEMO_KEY" )  # View the full comment text cat(comment_details$comment)  # Get details for multiple comments comment_ids <- c("FBI-2024-0001-0002", "FBI-2024-0001-0005",                   "FBI-2024-0001-0007")                   multiple_comments <- get_comment_details(   id = comment_ids,    api_keys = "DEMO_KEY" )  # Using multiple API keys for higher rate limits keys <- c("api_key_1", "api_key_2", "api_key_3") results <- get_comment_details(   id = comment_ids,    api_keys = keys )  # Typical workflow: get metadata first, then full details # Get metadata for comments on a document metadata <- get_commentsOnId(objectId = "09000064865d514a",                              api_keys = "DEMO_KEY") # Then fetch full details for specific comments of interest detailed <- get_comment_details(   id = metadata$id[1:10],  # First 10 comments   api_keys = "DEMO_KEY" ) } # }

what: Retrieve Comments on a Specific Document or from an Agency — get_commentsOnId
title: Retrieve Comments on a Specific Document or from an Agency — get_commentsOnId
text: Fetches comment metadata regulations.gov. Can retrieve comments specific document using objectId, comments submitted agency. Returns data frame containing comment details based specified filters.

title: Retrieve Comments on a Specific Document or from an Agency — get_commentsOnId
code: get_commentsOnId(objectId = NULL, agencyId = NULL,                   lastModifiedDate = Sys.time(),                   lastModifiedDate_mod = "le",                   api_keys)

title: Retrieve Comments on a Specific Document or from an Agency — get_commentsOnId
text: objectId Optional character string containing objectId document's metadata. provided, returns comments specific document. Must specify either objectId agencyId. agencyId Optional character string containing agency acronym. provided, returns comments submitted specified agency. Must specify either objectId agencyId. Must match official agency acronyms used regulations.gov (e.g., "EPA", "FCC", "CMS"). lastModifiedDate Filter comments last modified date. Default current system time. Format must : "yyyy-MM-dd%20HH:mm:ss" (e.g., "2024-01-01%2000:00:00" January 1, 2024). lastModifiedDate_mod Modifier date filter. Acceptable values: "le" Less equal (default) "ge" Greater equal NULL Collects results single exact date match api_keys Character string vector containing API key(s) api.data.gov. multiple keys provided, function cycle manage rate limits.

title: Retrieve Comments on a Specific Document or from an Agency — get_commentsOnId
text: Regulations.gov API Documentation: https://open.gsa.gov/api/regulationsgov/ Agency Acronym List: https://www.regulations.gov/agencies

title: Retrieve Comments on a Specific Document or from an Agency — get_commentsOnId
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get comments on a specific document using objectId comment_metadata <- get_commentsOnId(   objectId = "09000064865d514a",    api_keys = "DEMO_KEY" )  # Get all comments submitted to the EPA epa_comments <- get_commentsOnId(   agencyId = "EPA",   api_keys = "DEMO_KEY" )  # Get comments modified after a specific date recent_comments <- get_commentsOnId(   agencyId = "FCC",   lastModifiedDate = "2024-06-01%2000:00:00",   lastModifiedDate_mod = "ge" )  # Using multiple API keys for higher rate limits keys <- c("api_key_1", "api_key_2", "api_key_3") results <- get_commentsOnId(agencyId = "EPA", api_keys = keys) } # }

what: Retrieve Dockets from a Government Agency — get_dockets
title: Retrieve Dockets from a Government Agency — get_dockets
text: Fetches docket information specified government agency using regulations.gov API. function returns data frame containing docket details based specified filters.

title: Retrieve Dockets from a Government Agency — get_dockets
code: get_dockets(agency, lastModifiedDate = Sys.time(),              lastModifiedDate_mod = "le", docketType = NULL,              api_keys)

title: Retrieve Dockets from a Government Agency — get_dockets
text: agency character string containing agency acronym. Must match official agency acronyms used regulations.gov (e.g., "EPA", "FCC", "CMS"). lastModifiedDate Filter dockets last modified date. Default current system time. Format must : "yyyy-MM-dd%20HH:mm:ss" (e.g., "2024-01-01%2000:00:00" January 1, 2024). lastModifiedDate_mod Modifier date filter. Acceptable values: "le" Less equal (default) "ge" Greater equal NULL Collects results single exact date match docketType Optional filter docket type. Filter specific docket categories (e.g., "Rulemaking", "Nonrulemaking"). See regulations.gov documentation valid types. api_keys Character string vector containing API key(s) api.data.gov. multiple keys provided, function cycle manage rate limits.

title: Retrieve Dockets from a Government Agency — get_dockets
text: Regulations.gov API Documentation: https://open.gsa.gov/api/regulationsgov/ Agency Acronym List: https://www.regulations.gov/agencies

title: Retrieve Dockets from a Government Agency — get_dockets
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage with EPA epa_dockets <- get_dockets("EPA", api_keys = "DEMO_KEY")  # Filter by date (dockets modified since January 1, 2024) recent_dockets <- get_dockets("FCC",                                lastModifiedDate = "2024-01-01%2000:00:00",                               lastModifiedDate_mod = "ge")  # Filter by docket type with specific date range rulemaking_dockets <- get_dockets("CMS",                                   lastModifiedDate = "2024-06-01%2000:00:00",                                   lastModifiedDate_mod = "ge",                                   docketType = "Rulemaking")  # Using multiple API keys for higher rate limits keys <- c("api_key_1", "api_key_2", "api_key_3") results <- get_dockets("EPA", api_keys = keys) } # }

what: Retrieve Full Document Details and Metadata — get_document_details
title: Retrieve Full Document Details and Metadata — get_document_details
text: Fetches complete document details metadata regulations.gov using document IDs. Returns data frame containing full document information, including title, abstract, dates, associated metadata fields.

title: Retrieve Full Document Details and Metadata — get_document_details
code: get_document_details(id, lastModifiedDate = Sys.time(), api_keys)

title: Retrieve Full Document Details and Metadata — get_document_details
text: id Character string vector containing document ID(s) obtained get_documents search functions. Duplicate IDs automatically removed optimize API usage. lastModifiedDate Filter documents last modified date. Default current system time. Format must : "yyyy-MM-dd%20HH:mm:ss" (e.g., "2024-01-01%2000:00:00" January 1, 2024). api_keys Character string vector containing API key(s) api.data.gov. multiple keys provided, function cycle manage rate limits.

title: Retrieve Full Document Details and Metadata — get_document_details
text: function retrieves complete metadata regulatory documents, including Federal Register notices, proposed rules, final rules, supporting materials. Unlike get_documents returns summary information, function fetches full detailed record regulations.gov API. function encounters errors execution, save successfully retrieved documents temporary .rda file provide file path recovery.

title: Retrieve Full Document Details and Metadata — get_document_details
text: Regulations.gov API Documentation: https://open.gsa.gov/api/regulationsgov/

title: Retrieve Full Document Details and Metadata — get_document_details
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get details for a single document using its ID doc_details <- get_document_details(   id = "EPA-HQ-OAR-2025-1806-0001",    api_keys = "DEMO_KEY" )  # View the document abstract cat(doc_details$abstract)  # Get details for multiple documents doc_ids <- c("EPA-HQ-OAR-2025-1806-0001",               "ED-2022-OPE-0157-0001")               multiple_docs <- get_document_details(   id = doc_ids,    api_keys = "DEMO_KEY" )  # Using multiple API keys for higher rate limits keys <- c("api_key_1", "api_key_2", "api_key_3") results <- get_document_details(   id = doc_ids,    api_keys = keys )  # Typical workflow: get summary first, then full details # Get summary metadata for all documents in a docket summary <- get_documents(docketId = "ED-2022-OPE-0157",                          api_keys = "DEMO_KEY")                          # Then fetch full details for specific documents of interest detailed <- get_document_details(   id = summary$id,    api_keys = "DEMO_KEY" ) } # }

what: Retrieve Documents from a Docket or Agency — get_documents
title: Retrieve Documents from a Docket or Agency — get_documents
text: Fetches document metadata regulations.gov. Can retrieve documents within specific docket documents belonging agency. Returns data frame containing document details based specified filters.

title: Retrieve Documents from a Docket or Agency — get_documents
code: get_documents(agencyId = NULL, docketId = NULL,                lastModifiedDate = Sys.time(),                lastModifiedDate_mod = "le", documentType = NULL,                api_keys)

title: Retrieve Documents from a Docket or Agency — get_documents
text: agencyId Optional character string containing agency acronym. Can used alone retrieve documents agency, combination docketId filter documents within specific docket. Must match official agency acronyms used regulations.gov (e.g., "EPA", "FCC", "CMS"). docketId Optional character string containing docket ID. provided alone agencyId, returns documents belonging specified docket. lastModifiedDate Filter documents last modified date. Default current system time. Format must : "yyyy-MM-dd%20HH:mm:ss" (e.g., "2024-01-01%2000:00:00" January 1, 2024). lastModifiedDate_mod Modifier date filter. Acceptable values: "le" Less equal (default) "ge" Greater equal NULL Collects results single exact date match documentType Optional filter document type. Filter specific document categories (e.g., "Notice", "Rule", "Proposed Rule", "Supporting & Related Material"). See regulations.gov documentation valid types. api_keys Character string vector containing API key(s) api.data.gov. multiple keys provided, function cycle manage rate limits.

title: Retrieve Documents from a Docket or Agency — get_documents
text: Regulations.gov API Documentation: https://open.gsa.gov/api/regulationsgov/ Agency Acronym List: https://www.regulations.gov/agencies

title: Retrieve Documents from a Docket or Agency — get_documents
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic usage - get all documents from a specific docket docket_docs <- get_documents(docketId = "EPA-HQ-OPP-2023-0123",                              api_keys = "DEMO_KEY")  # Get all documents from an agency epa_documents <- get_documents(agencyId = "EPA", api_keys = "DEMO_KEY")  # Filter by date (documents modified since January 1, 2024) recent_docs <- get_documents(agencyId = "FCC",                              lastModifiedDate = "2024-01-01%2000:00:00",                             lastModifiedDate_mod = "ge")  # Filter by document type within a specific docket proposed_rules <- get_documents(docketId = "CMS-2024-0012",                                documentType = "Proposed Rule",                                lastModifiedDate = "2024-06-01%2000:00:00",                                lastModifiedDate_mod = "ge")  # Using multiple API keys for higher rate limits keys <- c("api_key_1", "api_key_2", "api_key_3") results <- get_documents(agencyId = "EPA", api_keys = keys) } # }

what: Search Regulations.gov Content by Keyword — get_searchTerm
title: Search Regulations.gov Content by Keyword — get_searchTerm
text: Searches specific term across regulations.gov content types. Returns documents, comments, dockets match search term, optional filters date, agency, parameters.

title: Search Regulations.gov Content by Keyword — get_searchTerm
code: get_searchTerm(searchTerm, endpoint,                 lastModifiedDate = Sys.time(),                 lastModifiedDate_mod = "le", agencyId = NULL,                 docketId = NULL, docketType = NULL,                 commentOnId = NULL, api_keys)

title: Search Regulations.gov Content by Keyword — get_searchTerm
text: searchTerm character string containing term search . Can include multiple keywords (e.g., "climate change"). URL-encoding handled automatically function. endpoint Character string specifying content type search within. Valid options: "documents" Search within regulatory documents "comments" Search within public comments "dockets" Search within docket metadata lastModifiedDate Filter results last modified date. Default current system time. Format must : "yyyy-MM-dd%20HH:mm:ss" (e.g., "2024-01-01%2000:00:00" January 1, 2024). lastModifiedDate_mod Modifier date filter. Acceptable values: "le" Less equal (default) "ge" Greater equal NULL Collects results single exact date match agencyId Optional character string filter agency acronym (e.g., "EPA", "FCC", "CMS"). docketId Optional character string filter specific docket ID. Limits search content within particular docket. docketType Optional character string filter docket type (e.g., "Rulemaking", "Nonrulemaking"). commentOnId Optional character string filter document ID comments responding . applicable endpoint = "comments". api_keys Character string vector containing API key(s) api.data.gov. multiple keys provided, function cycle manage rate limits.

title: Search Regulations.gov Content by Keyword — get_searchTerm
text: Regulations.gov API Documentation: https://open.gsa.gov/api/regulationsgov/ Agency Acronym List: https://www.regulations.gov/agencies

title: Search Regulations.gov Content by Keyword — get_searchTerm
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic search for documents about "climate change" climate_results <- get_searchTerm("climate change", api_keys = "DEMO_KEY")  # Search for comments containing "pesticide" from EPA epa_comments <- get_searchTerm("pesticide",                                 endpoint = "comments",                                 agencyId = "EPA")  # Search within a specific docket for recent documents docket_search <- get_searchTerm("safety",                                 endpoint = "documents",                                docketId = "EPA-HQ-OPP-2023-0123",                                lastModifiedDate = "2024-01-01%2000:00:00",                                lastModifiedDate_mod = "ge")  # Search for rulemaking dockets about healthcare healthcare_dockets <- get_searchTerm("healthcare",                                      endpoint = "dockets",                                     docketType = "Rulemaking")  # Using multiple API keys for higher rate limits keys <- c("api_key_1", "api_key_2", "api_key_3") results <- get_searchTerm("air quality",                           endpoint = "documents",                          api_keys = keys) } # }

what: Save Docket Metadata to Local Directory — save_dockets
title: Save Docket Metadata to Local Directory — save_dockets
text: Retrieves docket metadata specified agency(ies) saves structured local directory. Creates organized folder system storing managing regulatory data time.

title: Save Docket Metadata to Local Directory — save_dockets
code: save_dockets(agency, api_keys)

title: Save Docket Metadata to Local Directory — save_dockets
text: agency character string containing agency acronym. Must match official agency acronyms used regulations.gov (e.g., "EPA", "FCC", "CMS"). api_keys Character string vector containing API key(s) api.data.gov. multiple keys provided, function cycle manage rate limits.

title: Save Docket Metadata to Local Directory — save_dockets
text: function uses get_dockets internally fetch latest docket metadata, need API key.

title: Save Docket Metadata to Local Directory — save_dockets
